name: Texas Breakfast Tacos
recipeInstructions:
For recipe see Southwest Guacamole.
For recipe see Fresh Tomato Salsa.
Bell pepper should be seeded and cut into strips.
Tortillas should be 7 to 8 inches in diameter.
Use a mixture of shredded Colby and Monterey Jack cheeses.
Prepare Southwest Guacamole and Fresh Tomato Salsa; reserve.
Cook and stir the sausage, onion, and bell pepper strips in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat, stirring frequently, until the sausage is done, about 10 minutes. Drain and reserve.
Heat 1 tablespoon of margarine in a skillet over medium heat until hot and bubbly. Pour eggs into skillet. As the eggs begin to set at the bottom and sides, gently lift cooked portions with a spatula so that the thin uncooked portion can flow to the bottom. Avoid constant stirring. Cook until the eggs are thickened throughout but still moist, about 5 minutes.
Heat oven to 450 degrees F. Spoon about 1/4 cup sausage mixture onto each of the tortillas; top each with about 1/4 cup of the eggs and 2 tablespoons of the cheese mixture. Fold tortillas into halves. Arrange 5 assembled tacos in an ungreased jelly roll pan (15 1/2 x 10 1/2 x 1-inches); brush with melted margarine.
Bake until light golden brown, about 10 to 12 minutes. Repeat with remaining tacos. Serve with guacamole and salsa.
